title: Top B.Sc Medical Laboratry Technology College in Noida
content: The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer) General Medical Microbiology Basic Haematology Basic Clinical Biochemistry Human Anatomy and Physiology Systematic Bacteriology Basics of Haematological diseases Biochemical metabolism Fundamentals of Histology Blood Banking & Genetics Applied Bacteriology Applied Haematology Analytical Clinical Biochemistry Applied Histopathology Medical Mycology and Virology Immunology & Bacterial serology Applied Clinical Biochemistry Immunopathology & Molecular Biology Medical Parasitology & Entomology Advanced Haematology 01 Clinical Care in Medical lab Practice Apply core basic knowledge associated with the Medical Laboratory Technology profession which includes Ethics, Good Lab Practice, Quality Control and Patient Safety. 02 Communication in Medical lab Practice Develop an effective communication with patients, care-givers, other health professional’s members of community and procedures to perform to make an investigation reports. 03 Membership of a multidisciplinary health team Distinguish and communicate within the team, including transparency about aims, decisions, uncertainty and mistakes to work collaboratively and professionally to accomplish goal and accurate result for patient. 04 Ethics and accountability in medical lab Practice Apply ethical principles and commit to core concepts of clinical ethics and law to practice as healthcare service providers in the field of Medical Lab Technology. 05 Commitment to professional excellence Develop and execute professionalism to reflect in thought and action including technical competence, appearance, image, confidence level, empathy, compassion, understanding, patience, manners, verbal and non-verbal communication, an anti- discriminatory and non-judgmental attitude, and appropriate physical contact to ensure safe, effective and expected delivery of healthcare. 06 Leadership and mentorship Adapt leadership role where needed in order to ensure clinical significance and patient satisfaction and respond in an autonomous and confident manner to planned and uncertain errors or any other condition. 07 Social Accountability and Responsibility Assess and recognize that allied and healthcare professionals need to be advocates within the healthcare system, to judiciously manage resources. 08 Scientific attitude and Scholarship Adapt scientific and scholarly principles during interactions with patients and peers, educational endeavors, research activities and in all other aspects of professional lives. 09 Lifelong learning Adapt the ability to engage in independent and lifelong learning committed to the patient’s care and patient’s health. 01 Establish and maintain ethical standards of clinical laboratory as per regulations of accreditation bodies. 02 Undertake higher studies at any reputed institutions in the field of Medical and Allied sciences. 03 Evolve the new technologies and engage in entrepreneurship activities. The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer)
